title: AI What-If Simulation — Propose Configuration Changes and Project Downstream Impact
description: The AI Requirements Studio shall include a What-If Simulation workspace where users can propose benefit configuration changes and see projected downstream impact before committing. The simulation is read-only — results are always hypothetical until the user initiates the real change workflow.
disclaimer: Simulations are read-only projections. No data is changed until you explicitly initiate the configuration change workflow.

title: Pre-Configured What-If Scenarios for Demo — Waiting Period, Contribution, Eligibility
description: The What-If workspace shall include three pre-configured demonstration scenarios that immediately produce compelling impact projections from the seed data.

title: Impact Analysis Extended to Include Test Case Count and Compliance Implications
description: AI impact analysis (REQ-IMPACT-001) is extended to include two additional impact dimensions: (1) estimated test case count required for the change, (2) potential compliance implications (ACA, COBRA, ERISA) flagged for human review.

title: AI Test Case Generation — Requirements Produce Acceptance Criteria and Test Case Outlines
description: For each approved requirement, the AI shall be able to generate: (1) formal acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format, (2) test case outlines covering happy path, boundary conditions, and negative cases. Generated test cases are advisory — a human QA engineer reviews and finalizes them.

generationExample.requirement: Full-time employees working 30+ hours are eligible after 30 calendar days.

title: AI Backlog Prioritization — Score Requirements by Business Impact, Risk, and Dependencies
description: The AI shall provide an automated backlog prioritization pass that scores each requirement on: business impact, regulatory risk, employee impact count, estimated implementation effort, and dependency chain. Output is a ranked backlog with rationale for each prioritization decision.
outputFormat: Ranked list with: Requirement ID | Priority Score | Rationale | Dependencies

title: AI Compliance Assistant — Flag ACA, COBRA, ERISA Implications of Plan Changes
description: When a plan or eligibility configuration change is proposed, the AI Compliance Assistant shall analyze whether the change has potential ACA, COBRA, or ERISA implications. The assistant surfaces relevant flags for human compliance review — it does not make compliance determinations.
complianceDisclaimer: This is an informational analysis only. It does not constitute legal or compliance advice. All compliance implications require review by qualified legal and benefits counsel.

title: What-If Simulation Narrative: 'Change X → See Y Impact → Decide' User Flow
description: The What-If workspace user flow shall be: (1) User selects a configuration attribute to change, (2) User inputs the proposed new value, (3) AI runs impact analysis and presents a structured impact report, (4) User decides whether to initiate the real change workflow or discard the simulation. The simulation never modifies live data.
userFlow: → Initiate Change Workflow OR → Discard Simulation

title: AI Agentic Orchestration Concept Demo — Multi-Agent Change Pipeline
description: The demo shall include a showcase of the AI Agentic Orchestration concept: a single user instruction ('Change waiting period from 30 to 60 days') triggers a multi-step AI agent pipeline that produces a full impact assessment, configuration draft, and test case set for human review.

agentPipeline.action: Identifies all requirements referencing the 30-day waiting period
Finds all affected rules, plans, and enrollment workflows
Generates test cases for the proposed change

humanCheckpoint: All agent output is presented as a consolidated review package — human approves or modifies before any configuration change is applied
demoNote: This is a concept demonstration; in MVP all agent steps may be presented sequentially rather than truly parallel

title: Impact Analysis Visualized as Dependency Chain: PDF → REQ → RULE → PLAN → ENROLLMENT → CARRIER
description: The impact analysis results shall be visualized as a navigable dependency chain showing the full downstream path from a changed requirement. Each node in the chain is clickable to navigate to the affected entity's detail view.

title: What-If Simulation Results Exportable as Impact Report PDF
description: The results of a What-If simulation shall be exportable as a structured impact report suitable for stakeholder review. The report includes: proposed change, projected impact by dimension, compliance flags, recommended next steps, and the simulation timestamp.

title: AI Plan Comparison — Conversational Plan Selection Based on Profile
description: The Plan Comparison workspace shall include an AI mode where a user can describe an employee profile in natural language and the AI explains how the available plans compare for that profile, recommending the best fit with reasoning.

exampleInteraction.query: Which plan offers the best value for a family with moderate healthcare usage?
exampleInteraction.response: For a family with moderate usage, PPO-500 provides comprehensive coverage with a $500 deductible and full network flexibility. HDHP-3000 has lower employee premiums ($X less/month) but higher out-of-pocket exposure if care usage is above average. PPO-500 is recommended for moderate usage profiles.

title: Simulation Audit Trail — All What-If Runs Logged with User, Scenario, and Timestamp
description: Every What-If simulation run shall be logged to the audit trail: who ran it, what change was simulated, the impact summary results, and whether the simulation led to a real change being initiated. This creates an evidence trail of decision-making.
